channel.title: CyberNetSec - Other
channel.description: Latest other threat intelligence - the last 10 articles

item.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.4-Cyber, a Specialized AI Model for Defensive Cybersecurity
item.description:
OpenAI has introduced GPT-5.4-Cyber, a specialized version of its latest large language model fine-tuned for defensive cybersecurity tasks. The model features fewer restrictions than its public counterpart, enabling security professionals to perform complex operations like binary reverse engineering...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Security Operations, Threat Intelligence, Other

Read Full Article →

item.title: Bitcoin Depot Loses $3.6M in Crypto After Attackers Steal Settlement Account Credentials
item.description:
Bitcoin Depot, a major US operator of cryptocurrency ATMs, has disclosed a cyberattack that resulted in the theft of more than 50 Bitcoin (BTC), valued at over $3.6 million. According to the company, threat actors managed to steal credentials linked to its digital asset settlement accounts. Using th...

Severity: High

Categories: Cyberattack, Data Breach, Other

Read Full Article →

item.title: Job Seekers Targeted in Phishing Scam Impersonating Palo Alto Networks Recruiters
item.description:
Threat actors are conducting a sophisticated phishing campaign targeting senior-level professionals by impersonating recruiters from cybersecurity giant Palo Alto Networks. According to the company's own Unit 42 threat intelligence team, the scam uses data scraped from LinkedIn for highly personaliz...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Phishing, Policy and Compliance, Other

Read Full Article →

item.title: Anthropic Accidentally Leaks 'Claude Code' AI Source Code in Packaging Error
item.description:
AI research company Anthropic experienced a significant intellectual property leak after the full source code for its flagship 'Claude Code' AI tool was accidentally published. The leak was caused by a packaging error where a JavaScript source map file, included in a public npm package, contained th...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Data Breach, Policy and Compliance, Other

Read Full Article →

item.title: LinkedIn Accused of Secretly Scanning for 6,000+ Browser Extensions
item.description:
A new report from the user association Fairlinked e.V. alleges that LinkedIn is secretly scanning visitors' browsers for the presence of over 6,000 installed browser extensions. The practice, dubbed "BrowserGate," reportedly involves injecting hidden JavaScript to fingerprint users. The report claim...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Other

Read Full Article →

item.title: Novel Font-Rendering Trick Hides Malicious Commands from AI Assistants
item.description:
Security researchers have developed a novel technique that uses font-rendering manipulations to hide malicious commands from AI assistants and security scanners while they remain invisible to human users. Disclosed on March 18, 2026, the method exploits how web browsers render different fonts to cre...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Threat Intelligence, Other

Read Full Article →

item.title: Google Finalizes Acquisition of Cloud Security Firm Wiz to Bolster Multicloud Security
item.description:
Google has officially completed its acquisition of Wiz, a leading cloud-native security platform. This major strategic investment is aimed at enhancing Google Cloud's security offerings, particularly for customers operating in multicloud environments. Wiz will join the Google Cloud division but will...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Cloud Security, Policy and Compliance, Other

Read Full Article →

item.title: Zapier Pledges Free AI Education for One Million People to Lower Skills Barrier
item.description:
Workflow automation company Zapier has launched the "1 Million AIs" initiative, a public pledge to provide free AI education and training to one million people. The program aims to democratize AI skills, making them accessible to non-technical users and driving broader adoption of AI-powered automat...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Other, Policy and Compliance

Read Full Article →

item.title: China's CERT Warns 'OpenClaw' AI Model Can Be Abused to Delete Data, Expose Keys
item.description:
China's national Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT) has issued a significant security warning about the 'OpenClaw' AI model. According to the alert reported on March 12, 2026, the model can be manipulated to perform dangerous and destructive actions, such as deleting user data, exposing sensiti...

Severity: High

Categories: Vulnerability, Threat Intelligence, Other

Read Full Article →

item.title: CrowdStrike & Schwarz Digits to Offer Sovereign AI-Native Security on STACKIT Cloud
item.description:
CrowdStrike has announced a strategic partnership with Schwarz Digits, the IT division of the group owning retail giants Lidl and Kaufland. The collaboration will deliver CrowdStrike's AI-native Falcon cybersecurity platform on STACKIT, the sovereign cloud developed by Schwarz Group. This initiative...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Cloud Security, Policy and Compliance, Other

Read Full Article →
